incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect number of symbols on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Check the table and the key—each symbol stands for 2 books.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect number of symbols on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. James read 12 books—that's 6 symbols. Sofia read 7 books—that's 3 whole symbols and 1 half. Aiden read 11 books—that's 5 whole and 1 half.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect number of symbols three or more times—system models the answer

params.description: System places symbols: James gets 6 full symbols, Sofia gets 3 full + 1 half, Aiden gets 5 full + 1 half

steps.text: This is tricky, so let me show you. James read 12 books. Each symbol stands for 2 books, so 12 divided by 2 equals 6 symbols. Sofia read 7 books—that's 3 symbols for 6 books, plus a half-symbol for the extra 1 book. Aiden read 11 books—5 symbols for 10 books, plus a half for the last book.
